documentation:
# pdfnaut

[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pdfnaut/badge/?version=latest)](https://pdfnaut.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pdfnaut)
![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dw/pdfnaut)
![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pdfnaut)

> [!Warning]
> pdfnaut is currently in an early stage of development and has only been tested with a small set of compliant documents. Some non-compliant documents may work under strict=False. Expect bugs or issues.

pdfnaut aims to become a PDF processor for parsing PDF 2.0 files.

Currently, pdfnaut provides a low-level interface for reading and writing PDF objects as defined in the [PDF 2.0 specification](https://developer.adobe.com/document-services/docs/assets/5b15559b96303194340b99820d3a70fa/PDF_ISO_32000-2.pdf).

## Examples

The newer high-level API

```py
from pdfnaut import PdfDocument

pdf = PdfDocument.from_filename("tests/docs/sample.pdf")
first_page = next(pdf.flattened_pages)

if first_page.content_stream:
    print(first_page.content_stream.contents)
```

The more mature low-level API

```py
from pdfnaut import PdfParser

with open("tests/docs/sample.pdf", "rb") as doc:
    pdf = PdfParser(doc.read())
    pdf.parse()

    pages = pdf.trailer["Root"]["Pages"]

    first_page_stream = pages["Kids"][0]["Contents"]
    print(first_page_stream.decode())
```

classes.name: pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfParseError
documentation: The parser was unable to continue parsing the PDF

classes.name: pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfFilterError
documentation: A filter was unable to decode a stream or is not supported

classes.name: pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfResolutionError
documentation:
The document was unable to resolve a reference because no resolution method
is available.

classes.name: pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfWriteError
documentation: The writer was unable to serialize an object

modules.name: pdfnaut
documentation: pdfnaut is a Python library for reading and writing PDFs at a low level.

classes.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.from_filename
documentation: Loads a PDF document from a file `path`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.has_encryption
documentation: Whether this document includes encryption.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.catalog
documentation:
The root of the document's object hierarchy, including references to pages,
outlines, destinations, and other core attributes of a PDF document.

For details on the contents of the catalog, see `§ 7.7.2 Document Catalog`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.info
documentation:
The `Info` entry in the catalog which includes document-level information
described in `§ 14.3.3 Document information dictionary`.

Some documents may specify a metadata stream rather than an Info entry. This can be
accessed with {attr}`.PdfDocument.metadata`. PDF 2.0 deprecates all keys of this
entry except for `CreationDate` and `ModDate`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.pdf_version
documentation:
The version of the PDF standard used in this document.

The version of a PDF may be identified by either its header or the Version entry
in the catalog. If the Version entry is absent or the header specifies a later
version, the header version is returned. Otherwise, the Version entry is returned.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.metadata
documentation:
The Metadata entry of the catalog which includes document-level metadata
stored as XMP.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.page_tree
documentation:
The document's page tree. See `§ 7.7.3 Page Tree`.

For iterating over the pages of a PDF, prefer {attr}`.PdfDocument.flattened_pages`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.outline_tree
documentation:
The document's outlines commonly referred to as bookmarks.

See `§ 12.3.3 Document Outline`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.flattened_pages
documentation: A generator suitable for iterating over the pages of a PDF.

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.page_layout
documentation:
The page layout to use when opening the document. May be one of the following
values:

- SinglePage: Display one page at a time (default).
- OneColumn: Display the pages in one column.
- TwoColumnLeft: Display the pages in two columns, with odd-numbered pages
  on the left.
- TwoColumnRight: Display the pages in two columns, with odd-numbered pages
  on the right.
- TwoPageLeft: Display the pages two at a time, with odd-numbered
  pages on the left (PDF 1.5).
- TwoPageRight: Display the pages two at a time, with odd-numbered
  pages on the right (PDF 1.5).

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.page_mode
documentation:
Value specifying how the document shall be displayed when opened:

- UseNone: Neither document outline nor thumbnail images visible (default).
- UseOutlines: Document outline visible.
- UseThumbs: Thumbnail images visible.
- FullScreen: Full-screen mode, with no menu bar, window controls, or any
  other window visible.
- UseOC: Optional content group panel visible (PDF 1.5).
- UseAttachments: Attachments panel visible (PDF 1.6).

methods.name: pdfnaut.document.PdfDocument.language
documentation:
A language identifier that shall specify the natural language for all text in
the document except where overridden by language specifications for structure
elements or marked content (`§ 14.9.2 Natural language specification`).
If this entry is absent, the language shall be considered unknown.

A high-level interface over {class}`~.PdfParser`.

PDF authors who want to work with a document in a high-level way should use
this interface over `PdfParser`.

modules.name: pdfnaut.cos.encodings.pdfdoc
documentation:
charmap codec based on Annex D.3: PDFDocEncoding character set.

Implemented as seen in this example:
https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Lib/encodings/cp1252.py

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.PdfXRefSection
documentation:
A cross-reference section in a XRef table representing an incremental update.

Each section is comprised of one or multiple subsections containing XRef entries.
@dataclass
class PdfXRefTable:
"""A cross-reference table which permits random access to objects across a PDF
(``§ 7.5.4 Cross-reference table``).
It is compromised of cross-reference sections. Initially, a PDF document contains a
single section (or two if it's linearized). Additional sections are appended when the
document is incrementally updated (``§ 7.5.6 Incremental updates``).
sections: list[PdfXRefSection]

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.PdfXRefSubsection

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.PdfXRefSubsection.first_obj_number
documentation:
The object number of the first entry in this section. Each entry's object number 
starts here and is incremented by one.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.PdfXRefSubsection.count
documentation: The number of entries in this subsection.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.PdfXRefSubsection.entries
documentation: The entries contained in this subsection.
A cross-reference subsection in an XRef section.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.FreeXRefEntry

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.FreeXRefEntry.next_free_object
documentation: The object number of the next free object in the linked list.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.FreeXRefEntry.gen_if_used_again
documentation: The generation to apply to an object if this entry is used again.

A Type 0 (`f`) or free entry. Free entries are entries not currently in use and are
members of the linked list of free objects.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.InUseXRefEntry

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.InUseXRefEntry.offset
documentation: The byte offset of the object in the file (starting after the %PDF marker).

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.InUseXRefEntry.generation
documentation: The generation of the object.

A Type 1 (`n`) or in-use entry. In-use entries refer to the objects stored in a
document.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.CompressedXRefEntry

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.CompressedXRefEntry.objstm_number
documentation: The object number of the object stream containing this object.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.xref.CompressedXRefEntry.index_within
documentation: The index of the object within the object stream.

A Type 2 or compressed entry. Compressed entries refer to objects stored within
an object stream.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.containers.PdfDictionary
documentation:
An associative table containing pairs of objects (entries) where each entry is
composed of a key (a name object) and a value (any PDF object)
(`§ 7.3.7 Dictionary objects`).

{class}`PdfDictionary` is effectively a Python dictionary. Its keys are strings and
its values are any PDF object. The main difference from a typical dictionary is that
PdfDictionary automatically resolves references when indexing.

The underlying data in unresolved form is stored in {attr}`.PdfDictionary.data`.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.containers.PdfArray
documentation:
A heterogeneous collection of sequentially arranged items (`§ 7.3.6 Array objects`).

{class}`PdfArray` is effectively a Python list. The main difference from a typical list
is that PdfArray automatically resolves references when indexing.

The underlying data in unresolved form is stored in {attr}`.PdfArray.data`.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_header
documentation:
Appends the PDF file header to the document (`§ 7.5.2 File Header`).

Arguments:
: version (str):

  : A string representing the version of the PDF file.

  with_binary_marker (bool, optional):

  : Whether to also append the recommended binary marker. Defaults to True.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_object
documentation:
Appends an indirect object to the document.

Arguments:
: reference ({class}`.PdfReference` | {class}`tuple[int, int]`):

  : The object number and generation to which the object should be assigned.

  contents ({class}`.PdfObject` | {class}`.PdfStream`):

  : The contents to associate with the reference.

Returns:

: The offset where the indirect object starts.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.generate_xref_section
documentation:
Generates a cross-reference section from a list of `rows`.

Each row consists of a two-element tuple containing the object number of the
XRef entry and the entry itself. The object numbers will determine the amount
of subsections created and the entries within them.

The output is a list of XRef subsections that can be then serialized by either
{meth}`.write_standard_xref_section` or {meth}`.write_compressed_xref_section`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_standard_xref_section
documentation:
Appends a standard XRef section (`§ 7.5.4 Cross-Reference Table`) to the document.
Returns the `startxref` offset that should be written to the document.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_compressed_xref_section
documentation:
Appends a compressed XRef stream (`§ 7.5.8 Cross-Reference Streams`) from
`section` (to use as part of the extent) to the document.

Returns the `startxref` offset that should be written to the document.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_trailer
documentation:
Appends a standard `trailer` to the document (`§ 7.5.5 File Trailer`)
alongside the `startxref` offset.

Both arguments are optional, indicating their presence in the appended output.
If the XRef section written previously was an XRef stream, the trailer has
already been written and should be `None`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.serializer.PdfSerializer.write_eof
documentation: Appends the End-Of-File marker to the document.

A serializer that is able to produce a valid PDF document.

Arguments:
    eol (bytes, optional):
        The end-of-line to be used when serializing (CR, LF, or CRLF). Defaults to CRLF.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream.decode
documentation:
Returns the decoded contents of the stream. If no filter is defined,
it returns the original contents.

Raises {class}`.pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfFilterError` if a filter is unsupported.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream.create
documentation:
Creates a stream from unencoded data `raw` applying the filter(s) specified in
`details`. The length of the encoded output will automatically be appended
to `details`.

Raises {class}`.pdfnaut.exceptions.PdfFilterError` if a filter is unsupported.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream.details
documentation: The stream extent dictionary (`§ 7.3.8.2 Stream extent`).

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream.raw
documentation: The raw data which is assumed to be encoded.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.stream.PdfStream._crypt_params
documentation: Parameters specific to the Crypt filter

A sequence of bytes that may be of unlimited length. Objects with a large
amount of data like images or fonts are usually represented by streams
(`§ 7.3.8 Stream objects`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.date.PdfDate

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.date.PdfDate.from_pdf
documentation: Creates a {class}`PdfDate` from a PDF date string (for example: `D:20010727133720`).

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.date.PdfDate.from_datetime
documentation: Creates a {class}`PdfDate` from a {class}`datetime.datetime` object.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.date.PdfDate.as_datetime
documentation: Returns the {class}`datetime.datetime` equivalent of this date.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.date.PdfDate.as_pdf_string
documentation: Returns the PDF equivalent of this date as a string.
A date value stored in a PDF document (`§ 7.9.4 Dates`)

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.ContentStreamIterator
documentation:
An iterator designed to consume the operators of a content stream.

For each instruction in the stream, this iterator will yield a tuple including, in order,
the name of the operator and a list of operands.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.done
documentation: Whether the parser has reached the end of data.
* Scanning

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.skip
documentation: Skips/advances `n` characters in the tokenizer.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.peek
documentation: Peeks `n` characters into `data` without advancing through the tokenizer.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.peek_line
documentation:
Peeks from the current position until an EOL marker is found (not included
in the output).

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.consume
documentation: Consumes and returns `n` characters.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.matches
documentation: Checks whether `keyword` starts at the current position.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer._get_reference_if_matched
documentation:
Returns the Regex match if an indirect reference is at the current position
otherwise None.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.skip_if_matches
documentation:
Advances `len(keyword)` characters if `keyword` starts at the current
position. Returns whether the match was successful.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.skip_whitespace
documentation: Advances through PDF whitespace.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.skip_next_eol
documentation:
Skips the next EOL marker if matched. If `no_cr` is True, CR (`\r`) will
not be treated as a newline.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.skip_while
documentation:
Skips while `callback` returns True for an input character. If specified,
it will only skip `limit` characters. Returns how many characters were skipped.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.consume_while
documentation:
Consumes while `callback` returns True for an input character. If specified,
it will only consume up to `limit` characters.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.get_next_token
documentation: Parses and returns the next token.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_numeric
documentation:
Parses a numeric object.

PDF has two types of numbers: integers (40, -30) and real numbers (3.14). The range
and precision of these numbers may depend on the machine used to process the PDF.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_name
documentation:
Parses a name -- a uniquely defined atomic symbol introduced with a slash
and ending before a delimiter or whitespace.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_hex_string
documentation:
Parses a hexadecimal string. Hexadecimal strings usually include arbitrary binary
data. If the sequence is uneven, the last character is assumed to be 0.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_dictionary
documentation:
Parses a dictionary object. In a PDF, dictionary keys are name objects and
dictionary values are any object or reference. This parser maps name objects to
strings in this context.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_array
documentation: Parses an array. Arrays are heterogenous in PDF so they are mapped to Python lists.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_indirect_reference
documentation: Parses an indirect reference. Indirect references allow locating an object in a PDF.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_literal_string
documentation:
Parses a literal string. Literal strings may be entirely ASCII or may include
arbitrary binary data (usually when they are encrypted). This parser does not currently
distinguish between different encodings.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_comment
documentation: Parses a PDF comment. Comments have no syntactical meaning.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer.parse_operator
documentation:
Parses a PDF operator. Operators can be found in content streams and are only
parsed if {attr}`.parse_operators` is true.

A tokenizer designed to consume individual objects that do not depend on a cross
reference table. It is used by {class}`~pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser` for this purpose.

This tokenizer consumes basic objects such as arrays and dictionaries. Indirect objects
and streams depend on an XRef table and hence are not sequentially parsable. It is not
intended to parse these items but rather the objects stored within them.

Arguments:
: data (bytes):

  : The contents to be parsed.

  parse_operators (bool, optional):

  : Whether to also parse the operators present in content streams.
    Defaults to False.

functions.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.parse_text_string
documentation:
Parses a text string as defined in `§ 7.9.2.2 Text string type`.

Text strings may either be encoded in PDFDocEncoding, UTF-16BE, or (PDF 2.0) UTF-8.
Each encoding is indicated by a byte-order mark at the beginning (`þÿ`
for UTF-16BE and `ï»¿` for UTF-8). PDFDocEncoded strings have no such
mark.

functions.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.encode_text_string
documentation:
Encodes a text string to either PDFDocEncoding or UTF-16BE. Strings are encoded
with PDFDoc first then UTF-16BE if `text` cannot be encoded with PDFDoc.

If `utf8` is True, `text` will be encoded in UTF-8 as fallback instead of UTF-16BE.
Note that UTF-8 text strings is a PDF 2.0 feature which may not be supported by all
libraries.

variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.T
documentation: pytest complains if this is not here

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfNull
documentation: A PDF object representing nothing (`§ 7.3.9 Null Object`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfComment
documentation:
A comment introduced by the presence of the percent sign (`%`) outside a string or
inside a string. Comments have no syntactical meaning and shall be interpreted as
whitespace (`§ 7.2.4 Comments`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfName
documentation:
An atomic symbol uniquely defined by a sequence of 8-bit characters
(`§ 7.3.5 Name Objects`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfHexString

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfHexString.from_raw
documentation: Creates a hexadecimal string from ``data``

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfHexString.value
documentation: The decoded value of the hex string

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfHexString.raw
documentation: The hex value of the string

A PDF hexadecimal string which can be used to include arbitrary binary data in a PDF
(`§ 7.3.4.3 Hexadecimal Strings`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfReference

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfReference.get
documentation:
Returns the object this reference points to. If unable to resolve,
returns {exc}`.PdfResolutionError`
A reference to a PDF indirect object (`§ 7.3.10 Indirect objects`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.objects.base.PdfOperator

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.TrappedState.Unknown

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.title
documentation: The document's title.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.author
documentation: The name of the person who created the document.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.subject
documentation: The subject or topic of the document.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.keywords
documentation: Keywords associated with the document.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.creator
documentation:
If the document was converted to PDF from another format (ex. DOCX), the name of 
the PDF processor that created the original document from which it was converted 
(ex. Microsoft Word).

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.producer
documentation:
If the document was converted to PDF from another format (ex. PostScript), the name of 
the PDF processor that converted it to PDF (ex. Adobe Distiller).

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.creation_date_raw
documentation: The date and time the document was created, as a text string.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.modify_date_raw
documentation: The date and time the document was most recently modified, as a text string.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.creation_date
documentation: The date and time the document was created, in human-readable form.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.modify_date
documentation: The date and time the document was most recently modified, in human-readable form.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.trailer.Info.trapped
documentation:
A value indicating whether the document has been modified to include trapping
information (`§ 14.11.6 Trapping support`).

The document information dictionary (`§ 14.3.3 Document information dictionary`).

It represents document-level metadata and is stored in the trailer. Since PDF 2.0,
most of its function has been superseded by a document-level Metadata stream, with
exception of the CreationDate and ModDate keys.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.Required
documentation: Sentinel to mark a field as required.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.TextStringField
documentation: A field defining a key whose value is a text string (`§ 7.9.2.2 Text string type`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.EnumField
documentation: A field defining a key whose value is a set of names part of an enumeration.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.StandardField
documentation:
A field defining a key whose value is one of the following basic types: boolean,
numeric, arrays, dictionaries, names, streams, and null.

Text strings and dates have special handling and are better served by the
{class}`.TextStringField` and {class}`.DateField` classes respectively.

Names part of an enumeration are better served by the {class}`.EnumField` class.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.NameField
documentation: A field defining a key whose value is a name.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.FlagField
documentation: A field defining a key whose value is part of a set of bit flags.

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.fields.DateField
documentation: A field defining a key whose value is a date (`§ 7.9.4 Dates`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.kind
documentation: The type of annotation (`Table 171: Annotation types`)

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.rect
documentation: A rectangle specifying the location of the annotation in the page.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.contents
documentation:
The text contents that shall be displayed when the annotation is open, or if this
annotation kind does not display text, an alternate description of the annotation's 
contents.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.name
documentation: An annotation name uniquely identifying it among other annotations in its page.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.last_modified
documentation:
The date and time the annotation was most recently modified. This value should
be a PDF date string but processors are expected to accept any text string.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.flags
documentation: A set of flags specifying various characteristics of the annotation.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Annotation.language
documentation:
A language identifier that shall specify the natural language for all text in
the annotation except where overridden by other explicit language specifications
(`§ 14.9.2 Natural language specification`).

An annotation associates an object such as a note, link or rich media element
with a location on a page of a PDF document (`§ 12.5 Annotations`).

classes.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page

methods.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.content_stream
documentation: An iterator over the instructions producing the contents of this page.

methods.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.annotations
documentation: All annotations associated with this page (`§ 12.5 Annotations`)

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.resources
documentation:
Resources required by the page contents.

If the page requires no resources, this returns an empty resource dictionary.
If the page inherits its resources from an ancestor, this returns None.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.mediabox
documentation:
A rectangle specifying the boundaries of the physical medium in which the page
should be printed or displayed.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.cropbox
documentation: A rectangle specifying the visible region of the page.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.user_unit
documentation: The size of a user space unit, in multiples of 1/72 of an inch.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.rotation
documentation:
The number of degrees by which the page shall be visually rotated clockwise.
The value is a multiple of 90 (by default, 0).

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.objects.page.Page.metadata
documentation: A metadata stream, generally written in XMP, containing information about this page.

A page in the document (`§ 7.7.3.3 Page Objects`).

Arguments:
: size (tuple[int, int]):

  : The width and height of the physical medium in which the page should
    be printed or displayed.

classes.name: pdfnaut.security.providers.base.IdentityProvider
documentation: The Identity provider does nothing - same input, same output

modules.name: pdfnaut.security.providers._cryptodome
documentation: PyCryptodome

functions.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.pad_password
documentation:
Pads or truncates the input `password` to exactly 32 bytes.

- If `password` is longer than 32 bytes, it shall be truncated.
- If `password` is shorter than 32 bytes, it shall be padded by appending data from {const}`.PASSWORD_PADDING` as needed.

functions.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.get_value_from_bytes
documentation:
Returns the inner value of `contents` if it is an instance of {class}`.PdfHexString`,
otherwise returns `contents`

classes.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
: encryption ({class}`.PdfDictionary`):

  : The Standard encryption dictionary specified in the document's trailer.
    (`§ 7.6.4 Standard encryption dictionary`)

  ids ({class}`PdfArray[PdfHexString | bytes]`).

  : The ID array specified in the document's trailer.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.key_length
documentation: The length of the encryption key in bytes

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.compute_encryption_key
documentation:
Computes an encryption key from `password` according to Algorithm 2 in
`§ 7.6.4.3.1 File encryption key algorithm`

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.compute_owner_password
documentation:
Computes the O (`owner_password`) value in the Encrypt dictionary according
to Algorithm 3 in `§ 7.6.4.4 Password algorithms`.

As a fallback if there is no owner password, `user_password` is also specified.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.compute_user_password
documentation:
Computes the U (user password) value in the Encrypt dictionary according to
Algorithm 4 (rev. 2) and Algorithm 5 (rev. 3 and 4) in `§ 7.6.4.4 Password algorithms`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.authenticate_user_password
documentation:
Authenticates the provided user `password` according to Algorithms 6
(based on Algos. 4 and 5) in `§ 7.6.4.4 Password Algorithms`.

Returns:

: A tuple of two values: the encryption key that should decrypt the document and
  whether authentication was successful.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.authenticate_owner_password
documentation:
Authenticates the provided owner `password` (or user `password` if none)
according to Algorithm 7 (based on Algo. 3) in `§ 7.6.4.4 Password Algorithms`.

Returns:

: A tuple of two values: the encryption key that should decrypt the document and
  whether authentication was successful.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.compute_object_crypt
documentation:
Computes all needed parameters to encrypt or decrypt `contents` according to
`§ 7.6.3.1 Algorithm 1: Encryption of data using the RC4 and AES algorithms`.

This algorithm is only applicable for Encrypt versions 1-4 (deprecated in PDF 2.0).
Encrypt version 5 uses a simpler algorithm described in `§ 7.6.3.2 Algorithm 1.A`.

Arguments:
: encryption_key (bytes):

  : An encryption key generated by {meth}`.compute_encryption_key`

  contents ({class}`.PdfStream` | {class}`.PdfHexString` | bytes):

  : The contents to encrypt/decrypt. The type of object will determine what
    crypt filter will be used for decryption (StmF for streams, StrF for
    hex and literal strings).

  reference ({class}`.PdfReference`):

  : The reference of either the object itself (in the case of a stream) or
    the object containing it (in the case of a string).

  crypt_filter ({class}`.PdfDictionary`, optional):

  : The specific crypt filter to be referenced when decrypting the document.
    If not specified, the default for this type of `contents` will be used.

Returns:

: A tuple of 3 values: the crypt method to apply (AES-CBC or ARC4),
  the key to use with this method, and the data to encrypt/decrypt.

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.encrypt_object
documentation:
Encrypts the specified `contents` according to Algorithm 1 in
`§ 7.6.3 General Encryption Algorithm`.

For details on arguments, please see {meth}`.compute_object_crypt`

methods.name: pdfnaut.security.standard_handler.StandardSecurityHandler.decrypt_object
documentation:
Decrypts the specified `contents` according to Algorithm 1 in
`§ 7.6.3 General Encryption Algorithm`.

For details on arguments, please see {meth}`.compute_object_crypt`

An implementation of `§ 7.6.4 Standard security handler`

The Standard security handler includes access permissions and allows up to 2 passwords:
the owner password which has all permissions and the user password which should only
have the permissions specified by the document.

functions.name: pdfnaut.filters.batched
documentation:
Consumes `iterable` and yields batches of `n` elements (where `n` is an
integer greater than 1) until the iterator is exhausted.

If `strict` is True, each batch must include exactly `n` elements, raising a
`ValueError` otherwise.

Example:

: batched('ABCDEFG', 3) → ABC DEF G
itertools recipe

classes.name: pdfnaut.filters.ASCIIHexFilter
documentation:
Filter for hexadecimal strings (`§ 7.4.2 ASCIIHexDecode Filter`). EOD is `>`.

This filter does not take any parameters. `params` will be ignored.

classes.name: pdfnaut.filters.ASCII85Filter
documentation:
Filter for Adobe's ASCII85 implementation (`§ 7.4.3 ASCII85Decode Filter`). EOD is `~>`.

This filter does not take any parameters. `params` will be ignored.

classes.name: pdfnaut.filters.RunLengthFilter
documentation:
Filter for a form of Run-Length Encoding or RLE (`§ 7.4.5 RunLengthDecode Filter`)

In this filter, data is formatted as a sequence of runs. Each run starts with a length
byte and is followed by 1 to 128 bytes of data.

- If the length byte is in the range 0 to 127, the following `length byte + 1` bytes shall be copied exactly.
- If the length byte is in the range 129 to 255, the following byte shall be copied `257 - length` bytes.
- A length byte of 128 means EOD.

This filter does not take any parameters. `params` will be ignored.

classes.name: pdfnaut.filters.FlateFilter
documentation:
Filter for zlib/deflate compression (`§ 7.4.4 LZWDecode and FlateDecode Filters`).

This filter supports predictors which can increase predictability of data and hence
improve compression. 2 predictor groups are supported: the PNG filters specified in
`§ 9. Filtering` of the PNG spec and TIFF Predictor 2 in the TIFF 6.0 spec.

The predictor is specified by means of the Predictor key in `params` (default: 1).
If the Predictor is not 1, the following parameters can be provided:

- **Colors**: Amount of color components per sample. Can be any value greater than 1 (default: 1).
- **BitsPerComponent**: Bit length of each of the color components. Possible values are: 1, 2, 4, 8 (default), and 16.
- **Columns**: Amount of samples per row. Can be any value greater than 1 (default: 1).

Given these values, the length of a sample in bytes is given by

: `Length(Sample) = ceil((Colors * BitsPerComponent) / 8)`

and the length of a row is given by

: `Length(Row) = Length(Sample) * Columns`

classes.name: pdfnaut.filters.CryptFetchFilter
documentation:
Filter for encrypted streams (`§ 7.4.10 Crypt Filter`)

This filter takes two optional parameters: `Type`, which defines the decode parameters
as being for this filter; and `Name`, which defines what filter should be used to
decrypt the stream.

This filter requires 3 additional parameters. These parameters are for use exclusively
within the PDF processor and shall not be written to the document.

- **Handler**: An instance of the security handler.
- **EncryptionKey**: The encryption key generated from the security handler.
- **Reference**: The indirect reference of the object to decrypt.
TODO: Please test

functions.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.generate_file_id
documentation: Generates a file identifier as described in `§ 14.4 File identifier`.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PermsAcquired

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PermsAcquired.NONE
documentation: No permissions acquired, document is still encrypted.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PermsAcquired.USER
documentation: User permissions within the limits specified by the security handler.

class_variables.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PermsAcquired.OWNER
documentation: Owner permissions (all permissions).
Permissions acquired after opening or decrypting a document.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap.fill
documentation: Fills the object map with the items available in the PDF's xref table.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap._make_ref
documentation: Creates a new reference based on the current object number of the map.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap.add
documentation: Adds a new object to the map. Returns its reference.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap.delete
documentation:
Deletes object with number `obj_num`. Returns the object if it exists,
otherwise returns None.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.ObjectMap.free
documentation: Marks object with number `obj_num` as a free object.

A mapping of object numbers to either object references, in-use objects or free objects.

Object references included in {attr}`.ObjectMap.unresolved` are items that have not been
requested yet. Once an object is requested, it is removed from the unresolved set and
added to the map as is.

Free objects are indicated with the {class}`.FreeObject` class.

classes.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse
documentation:
Parses the entire document.

It begins by parsing the most recent XRef table and trailer. If this trailer
points to a previous XRef, this function is called again with a `start_xref`
offset until no more XRefs are found.

It also sets up the Standard security handler for use in case the document
is encrypted.

Arguments:
: start_xref (int, optional):

  : The offset where the most recent XRef can be found.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_header
documentation: Parses the %PDF-n.m header that is expected to be at the start of a PDF file.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.build_xref_map
documentation: Creates a dictionary mapping references to XRef entries in the document.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.get_merged_xrefs
documentation:
Combines all XRef updates in the document into a cross-reference mapping
that includes all entries.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.lookup_xref_start
documentation: Scans through the PDF until it finds the XRef offset then returns it

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_xref_and_trailer
documentation:
Parses both the cross-reference table and the PDF trailer.

PDFs may include a typical uncompressed XRef table (and hence separate XRefs and
trailers) or an XRef stream that combines both.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_simple_trailer
documentation:
Parses the PDF's standard trailer which is used to quickly locate other
cross reference tables and special objects.

The trailer is separate if the XRef table is standard (uncompressed).
Otherwise it is part of the XRef object.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_simple_xref
documentation:
Parses a standard, uncompressed XRef table of the format described in
`§ 7.5.4 Cross-Reference Table`.

If `startxref` points to an XRef object, {meth}`.parse_compressed_xref`
should be called instead.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_compressed_xref
documentation:
Parses a compressed cross-reference stream which includes both the XRef table
and information from the PDF trailer.

Described in `§ 7.5.8 Cross-Reference Streams`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_indirect_object
documentation:
Parses an indirect object not within an object stream, or basically, an object
that is directly referred to by an `xref_entry` and a `reference`

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.parse_stream
documentation: Parses a PDF stream of length ``extent``

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.get_object
documentation:
Resolves a reference into the indirect object it points to.

Arguments:
: reference ({class}`.PdfReference` | {class}`tuple[int, int]`):

  : A {class}`.PdfReference` object or a tuple of two integers representing,
    in order, the object number and the generation number.

  cache (bool, optional):

  : Whether to interact with the object store when resolving references.
    Defaults to True.

    When True, the parser will read entries from the object store and write new
    ones if they are not present. If False, the parser will always fetch new
    entries and will not write to the object store.

    Note that the object store will be accessed regardless of the value of
    cache\`\`if the object is new and is not included in the xref table.

Returns:

: The object the reference resolves to.

  If the reference is invalid (i.e. does not exist), returns {class}`.PdfNull`.
  If the object referred to is a free object, returns {class}`.FreeObject`.

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.decrypt
documentation:
Decrypts this document through the Standard security handler using the
provided `password`.

The standard security handler may specify 2 passwords: an owner password and a user
password. The owner password would allow full access to the PDF and the user password
should allow access according to the permissions specified in the document.

Returns:

: A {class}`.PermsAcquired` specifying the permissions acquired by `password`.

  - If the document is not encrypted, defaults to {attr}`.PermsAcquired.OWNER`
  - if the document was not decrypted, defaults to {attr}`.PermsAcquired.NONE`

methods.name: pdfnaut.cos.parser.PdfParser.save
documentation: Saves the contents of this parser to `filename`.

A parser that can completely parse a PDF document.

It consumes the PDF's cross-reference tables and trailers. It merges the tables
into a single one and provides an interface to individually parse each indirect
object using {class}`~pdfnaut.cos.tokenizer.PdfTokenizer`.

Arguments:
: data (bytes):

  : The document to be processed.

Keyword Arguments:
: strict (bool, optional):

  : Whether to warn or fail on issues caused by non-spec-compliance.
    Defaults to False.
